
```{python}
# we define ref_type in the main qmd file
import yaml
from IPython.display import display, Markdown, display_latex

from datetime import datetime, date

def date_constructor(loader, node):
    value = loader.construct_scalar(node)
    
    # Adjust the format string based on your date format
    if isinstance(value, int):
        return date(value, 1, 1)
    elif '-' in value:
        if len(value) == 7:
            return datetime.strptime(value + '-01', '%Y-%m-%d').date()
        elif len(value) == 10:
            return datetime.strptime(value, '%Y-%m-%d').date()
    
    raise ValueError(f"Invalid date format: {value}")


yaml.add_constructor('tag:yaml.org,2002:date', date_constructor, yaml.SafeLoader)




with open("data/boehm-fixed.yaml", "r") as file:
    data = yaml.load(file, Loader=yaml.SafeLoader)

n_references = len(data["references"])
bibs = data["references"]

# Convert integer years and year-month to datetime.date objects
#for article in bibs:
#    if isinstance(article['issued'], int):
#        article['issued'] = date(article['issued'], 1, 1)
#    elif len(article['issued']) == 6:
#        article['issued'] = datetime.strptime(article['issued'], '%Y-%m').date()
#sorted_bibs = sorted(bibs, key=lambda x: x['issued'] if isinstance(x['issued'], date) else datetime.strptime(str(x['issued']), '%Y-%m-%d').date(), reverse=True)
# Replace 'issued' values with datetime.date objects
for article in bibs:
    if isinstance(article['issued'], int):
        article['issued'] = date(article['issued'], 1, 1)
    if isinstance(article['issued'], str):
        if len(article['issued']) == 7:
            article['issued'] = datetime.strptime(article['issued'] + '-01', '%Y-%m-%d').date()
        elif len(article['issued']) == 10:
            article['issued'] = datetime.strptime(article['issued'], '%Y-%m-%d').date()



sorted_bibs = sorted(bibs, key=lambda x: x['issued'] if isinstance(x['issued'], date) else datetime.strptime(str(x['issued']) + '-01', '%Y-%m-%d').date(), reverse=True)



for ref_number in range(n_references):
    ref = sorted_bibs[ref_number]
    if (ref["type"] == ref_type): 
        title = ref["title"]
        # organize authors for printing
        author_yaml = ref["author"]
        n_authors = len(author_yaml)
        for i in range(n_authors):
            author_value = author_yaml[i]
            if "literal" in author_value:
                full_name = author_value["literal"]
            else:
                author_last = author_value["family"]
                author_first = author_value["given"]
                if author_last == "Boehm":
                    author_last = "**Boehm**"
                    author_first = "**{}**".format(author_first)
                author = author_first + " " + author_last
            if i == 0:
                authors = author
            elif i == n_authors - 1:
                authors = authors + ", and " + author
            else:
                authors = authors + ", " + author
            # ie, if the citation is of the type we want to print right now, here
        if ref_type == "article-journal":  # need to have correct keys for each type!!
            # organize other info for printing
            date = ref["issued"]
            doi = ref["doi"]
            journal = ref["container-title"]
            #volume = ref["volume"]
            page = ref["page"]
            formatted_text = f"\\footnotesize{authors} ({date}). {title}. *{journal}* {page}."
            # Print the formatted content as Markdown
            display(Markdown(formatted_text))
        if ref_type == "manuscript":
            date = "submitted"
            formatted_text = f"\\footnotesize{authors} ({date}). {title}."
        if ref_type == "thesis":
            date = ref["issued"]
            formatted_text = f"\\footnotesize{authors} ({date}). {title}."
        if ref_type == "book":
            date = ref["issued"]
            authors = authors + ", editors"
            formatted_text = f"\\footnotesize{authors} ({date}). *{title}*."
```
